nativeRange.habitat: Reef slopes, lagoon areas, and shallow reef zones from just below the surface down to approximately 40 meters; commonly found encrusting on rock and rubble in moderate-to-high flow areas

decorPreferences: space from neighboring corals to prevent encrusting contact

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Despite small polyps, Psammocora has been observed to be a surprisingly capable feeder and generally benefits from supplemental target feeding with fine coral foods — finely ground powders, phytoplankton, rotifers, oyster eggs, or amino acid blends — roughly 1–2 times per week. Some sources note it can also accept mysis shrimp. Supplemental feeding tends to boost growth rate and color intensity.

avoidWith: fast-encrusting corals (Xenia, Green Star Polyp) placed nearby
aggressive LPS corals with sweeper tentacles
corals placed in direct physical contact

imageCandidates.notes: Ryan McMinds; ~1024x768. Psammocora haimiana: sharp, well-lit complete in-situ colony showing the columnar knobby growth form and fine petaloid ('cat's paw') surface texture. Best whole-subject genus representative.
Ryan McMinds; 2599x2295. Psammocora stellata: sharp in-water lobed/columnar colony, complete and well-framed. Adds a distinct species.
Philippe Bourjon; 1200x875. Psammocora sp.: tack-sharp close view of the diagnostic petaloid 'cat's paw' polyp texture that defines the genus.
Philippe Bourjon; 1200x928. Psammocora profundacella: sharp encrusting colony showing the meandering 'galaxy' surface pattern. Distinct species.
Chaloklum Diving; 820x750. Psammocora digitata: in-water columnar/digitate growth form; slightly turbid water but clearly diagnostic. Distinct species.

summary: Psammocora Coral is a genus of encrusting small-polyp stony (SPS) corals from the Indo-Pacific, valued for delicate surface texture and color morphs ranging from greens and purples to multicolor 'galaxy' patterns. It is generally considered one of the more forgiving SPS options for established reef systems.
careNotes:
Psammocora is a genus with around eight or more described species, though trade specimens are rarely identified to species level, so it is typically discussed at the genus level in the hobby.

Psammocora is generally considered one of the more approachable SPS corals — more tolerant of parameter fluctuations than Acropora, though still requiring a stable, established reef system. It tends to suit intermediate-level SPS keepers who want an encrusting SPS with good growth and color variety.

Key care points:
• Lighting: Generally advised in the 100–250 PAR range. Sources suggest 150–250 PAR as a comfortable mid-range; the coral can survive at lower light (50–100 PAR) but color and growth suffer. New specimens should be acclimated gradually to avoid bleaching. Deep-water wild-collected morphs may prefer the lower end of this range. Strong blue/violet spectrum (420–460 nm) tends to bring out fluorescent coloration.
• Flow: Moderate is the most consistently cited requirement — sufficient to prevent detritus settling on the colony surface and to deliver nutrients, but not so intense as to erode tissue. Chaotic, indirect flow patterns are preferable to direct laminar blasting.
• Placement: Can encrust on virtually any hard substrate — rocks, flat ledges, vertical faces, and even overhangs have been documented. It will spread steadily in all directions, so adequate spacing from neighboring corals is important to prevent overgrowth conflicts. It is more likely to be the coral getting crowded out than the aggressor.
• Growth and fragging: Growth rate has been described as moderate — faster than Acropora in some accounts, but comparable to Montipora. In well-maintained systems, colonies have been reported to grow 3–4 inches (7–10 cm) annually. Regular fragging is advisable to control spread. Frags recover readily under moderate light and flow.
• Water chemistry: Standard SPS parameters apply — calcium 380–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1400 ppm, alkalinity 8–10 dKH. Psammocora has been noted as more tolerant than Acropora of brief parameter swings (e.g., a 0.5 dKH alkalinity drop is less likely to trigger tissue necrosis), though sustained instability still causes bleaching and die-off.
• Calcium and alkalinity supplementation will become necessary as colonies grow.
• Color morphs: Vivid trade morphs exist in greens, yellows, purples, and multicolor 'galaxy'/'space' patterns. Coloration is influenced by both lighting spectrum and dissolved nutrient levels.
breedingNotes: Fragging is the primary propagation method in captivity. Colonies have been reported to frag readily — pieces as small as 1–2 cm can recover on frag plugs or rubble under moderate light and flow. Sexual reproduction via broadcast spawning occurs in the wild but is not practiced in the hobby.

primary.name: LiveAquaria (SPS Corals section — Psammocora)
primary.notes: LiveAquaria did not have a dedicated Psammocora species page accessible at time of research; primary source role transferred to Tidal Gardens per fallback protocol.

additional.name: Tidal Gardens – Psammocora Coral Care
additional.notes: Used as de facto primary source. Provided calcium (~425 ppm), alkalinity (8–9 dKH), magnesium (~1350 ppm), PAR 100–200, medium-high flow, no sweeper tentacles, Indo-Pacific native range (Fiji, Tonga, Solomon Islands, GBR). Primary firsthand husbandry observations from coral farm.

additional.name: Manta Systems – Psammocora Coral Care Guide
additional.notes: Cross-checked high-stakes fields. Temperature 76–78°F (24.4–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.025–1.026, alkalinity 8–9.5 dKH, calcium 420–450 ppm, magnesium 1300–1400 ppm, PAR 120–250, moderate-to-high flow, mid-to-lower placement, beginner-friendly SPS, feeding 1–2x/week, depth to 40m. Agreed with Tidal Gardens on most parameters.

additional.name: Bulk Reef Supply – WWC Deep Space Psammocora (World Wide Corals)
additional.notes: Cross-checked parameters from WWC-branded product. Temperature 75–78°F (23.9–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 1.024–1.026, alkalinity 8–9 dKH, calcium 380–450 ppm, magnesium 1300–1450 ppm, PAR 150–250 medium, moderate flow, peaceful temperament. Consistent with other sources.

additional.name: Aquifarm – Psammocora Coral Care
additional.notes: Cross-checked parameters. Temperature 23–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 32–35 ppt (≈1.0246–1.0269 SG), alkalinity 8–12 dKH, calcium 350–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm, PAR 100–200, moderate-to-high flow. Minimum tank 30 gallons (~113L). Noted growth rate 3–4 inches/year. Minor disagreement: described as 'moderately aggressive with stinging capability' — disagrees with Tidal Gardens and BRS/WWC which say peaceful/no sweepers. Majority-peaceful assessment adopted.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn – ORA Kelly Green Psammocora
additional.notes: Cross-checked parameters for an aquacultured specimen. Temperature 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C), pH 8.1–8.4, salinity 32–35 ppt, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, calcium 350–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm, low-to-high PAR with best display at medium-high, moderate-to-strong flow, placement anywhere. Originated from Palau wild collection 1994. Consistent with primary source on most parameters; wider alkalinity and calcium ranges averaged.
